title: mssql connect with options.trustServerCertificate: true — the SQL Server TLS certificate is not validated (MITM)

params.passDetail: The mssql connection validates the server certificate (options.trustServerCertificate is not true) — the database connection's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: mssql.connect is configured with options: { trustServerCertificate: true }, which makes the driver trust ANY certificate the SQL Server presents. A network attacker can present a forged certificate and man-in-the-middle the connection — every query, credential, and record is exposed. Remove trustServerCertificate: true; install and trust the server's real CA instead.
params.absentCallDetail: No mssql connect call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: mssql.connect does not set options.trustServerCertificate; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: trustServerCertificate: true is the Azure SQL / SQL Server equivalent of rejectUnauthorized: false, pasted from a 'fix the self-signed cert error' answer. It ships and disables MITM protection on the database connection.
